YouTube Title: Bossam and Cold Noodles — and Pepper Actually Helps?! 🐷
YouTube Description:
Chef Ginger puts together the ultimate Korean summer set — tender boiled pork (bossam) wrapped in cabbage with ssamjang, served alongside icy cold buckwheat noodles in chilled broth. Hot and cold, side by side. But the real surprise? For the very first time, Pepper doesn't steal a thing — she actually helps, hauling over a whole head of cabbage and earning a rare grateful head pat. Full recipe in the pinned comment.

Subscribe for more of Chef Ginger & Pepper 🐾

#Shorts #CatChef #Bossam #ColdNoodles #CookingCat #ChefGinger #고양이 #고양이먹방 #요리하는고양이 #보쌈 #냉모밀
Comment:
📌 Full recipe — save it for your next summer dinner! 🔥❄️

🐷🍜 Chef Ginger's Bossam & Cold Buckwheat Noodles (serves 2–3)

🐷 BOSSAM (warm pork wraps)
1. Fill a pot with water to cover 600g pork belly. Add 2 tbsp soybean
   paste (doenjang), 1 green onion, 5 garlic cloves, 4 slices ginger,
   1/2 onion, 1 tsp peppercorns, 2 bay leaves. (Optional: 1 tsp instant
   coffee or a splash of soju to chase the porky smell away.)
2. Add the raw pork, cover, and gently simmer 40–50 min — until a
   chopstick slides right through.
3. Rest 5 min, then slice thick.
4. Build your wrap: cabbage leaf + a dab of ssamjang + a slice of pork
   + a little garlic. Fold and enjoy in one happy bite.

🍜 COLD BUCKWHEAT NOODLES (naeng-momil)
1. Boil 2 portions of buckwheat noodles (3–5 min), stirring gently.
2. Rinse hard under cold water, then shock in ice water till cold and
   springy.
3. Curl into a little nest in a chilled bowl.
4. Pour over chilled broth (or mix 1.5 cups water + 1/4 cup soy sauce
   + 2 tbsp mirin and chill), add ice, and top with seaweed, scallion,
   radish sprouts and a dot of wasabi.

Hot pork + icy noodles, together — that's the whole trick. 🔥❄️
